title: Hypertension in Diabetes: Initial Pharmacotherapy
text: Adults with diabetes and stage 1 hypertension are commonly started on ACE inhibitors or ARBs due to blood pressure control and kidney protection benefits. Lifestyle interventions remain foundational, including sodium reduction and exercise.

title: Atrial Fibrillation and Stroke Prevention
text: For non-valvular atrial fibrillation, anticoagulation is recommended based on CHA2DS2-VASc risk. Direct oral anticoagulants are generally preferred when not contraindicated because they reduce stroke risk and have practical dosing advantages.

title: Adult Major Depressive Disorder Management
text: First-line pharmacotherapy for many adults with major depressive disorder includes SSRIs due to efficacy and tolerability. Treatment selection should also consider patient preference, comorbidity, and prior response.

title: Type 2 Diabetes Lifestyle and Medication Overview
text: Initial management includes nutrition optimization, physical activity, and metformin in many patients unless contraindicated. Glycemic targets and therapy escalation are individualized using comorbid conditions and risk profile.

title: Community-Acquired Pneumonia in Adults
text: Outpatient treatment for uncomplicated community-acquired pneumonia may include guideline-recommended oral antibiotics with follow-up for worsening symptoms. Local resistance patterns and patient risk factors influence regimen choice.
